commit_scope: Keep the active Form stdlib and kernels on the object-BMF path: add reversible Form cells, an object-only BMF runtime, a shared binary artifact loader, a shared compiler object layer, Form-native BML compiler/runtime support, Form-native BMF grammar slices, a dialect-described shared source scanner, a runtime-extendable capability registry for grammars/codecs/I/O transforms, a sibling-kernel parity gate for Form workloads, and seedbanks for useful not-yet-paired surfaces that need transformation rather than erasure.

local_validation.summary: Translator architecture moved above executable target lists and out of string-tag identity. engine.fk now carries domain, lens, domain-kind, surface, contract, reverse-lens, and translation-space identity as binary Form nodes; surface names and domain/lens words no longer enter contract nodes. It also adds a generic form-adapter registry and planner where adapter capsules mint local Form identity roots, dynamic kinds, dynamic refs, surfaces, and capability nodes for any parser, grammar, encoder, decoder, compressor, decompressor, interface, or protocol family. The new recipe capsule ABI lifts this into a generic discovery/execution unit: capability contracts, measured costs, proof recipes, specializers, recipe capsules, and recipe registries are all Form nodes attached beside executable capabilities. recipe-capsule-abi-band.fk proves cost-scored discovery under effect/reversibility/losslessness/determinism constraints, proof lookup, specializer lookup/execution, flexible planning, and execution at 11100 in source and binary modes. dynamic-adapter-registry-band.fk defines separate private language and codec capsules, then proves direct registry selection, fixed route planning, flexible surface-driven planning, and compiled flexible-plan execution across form -> text -> compressed -> decompressed text -> restored form at 32500 in source and binary modes without adding domain/media vocabulary to engine.fk. The Go, Rust, and TypeScript kernel trace surfaces now report named Form recipe counts and named native primitive counts in addition to arm/variant counts. A traced python-bmf.fk source-compiler run identified fsc-string-to-bytes-loop, fsc-find-string-at?, fsc-find-string-from, and fsc-find-char-from as the dominant traced recipes, with str_len, char_at, str_eq, ord, and cons as the dominant traced natives. Length-specialized Form scanner recipes in source-compiler.fk reduced str_len from 495401 to 16551, ACCESS from 942270 to 463420, FNCALL from 1586074 to 1108546, and total walks from 7227308 to 6755829 for the same python-bmf.fk compile. The stronger JIT boundary is now write_file_text: a paired Go/Rust/TypeScript host I/O primitive that lets text compilers write text directly while binary codecs keep write_file_bytes. The same python-bmf.fk compile trace moved from the original 69.1s baseline and the 77.5s length-specialized run to 0.65s, with total walks at 5002131, FNCALL at 695910, ACCESS at 257103, ord at 748, and cons absent from top natives. source-compiler.fk now adds generic fsc-repo-file, fsc-repo-corpus, fsc-repo-document, fsc-repo-meaning, and meaning-field objects so a corpus carries boundary path/media labels while the stored Form binary carries local file-kind nodes, decoded structure, coordinate ranges without copied span text, and queryable decoder fields. Transport bytes/text are decoder input only; the persisted node is meaning. repo-file-ingestion-band.fk is written in form.action and proves ingestion of ten real repo files covering Python, Go, TypeScript, Rust, Form, Markdown, JSON, SVG, PNG, and JPEG at 35150 in source and binary modes. Binary import now follows the NUMS module-boundary lesson: raw pkg=0 ids are file-local, so the Go, Rust, and TypeScript binary loaders remap them through a per-import scope instead of trusting their numeric tuple across files. binary-import-local-remap.fk proves that the same raw local id maps consistently inside one imported binary and maps differently when the same bytes are imported again, at 1000 in source and binary modes. source-compiler.fk no longer treats form.action function-block bodies as physical-line units: it scans semicolon-delimited statements with quote and paren depth awareness, then normalizes whitespace outside quoted strings before expression lowering. form-action-migrated-bmf-band.fk and form-bml-maintenance-dialect.fk remain paired in source and binary modes after that scanner change. The sibling kernels now expose only generic host file I/O for stream support, file_size and file_byte_at, and bmf-byte-cursor-source-band.fk proves a Form-owned BMF cursor over a real repo file as path, offset, line, and column state. The proof advances bytes without recursive whole-file traversal, stores source ranges as offset and coordinate data without copied span text, keeps a multi-line BML expression in the source, and passes at 2800 in source and binary modes. The active BMF scanner repair has started: source-compiler.fk now has a Form-owned source cursor and scanner that emits one form-action BMF object plus the next cursor, with source coordinates attached to each object. bmf-source-scanner-rule-band.fk proves scanned source objects feed the existing BMF object rules directly, reducing scanned if/name/keyword objects into a Form recipe node at 4500 in source and binary modes. JSON, Markdown, and YAML now have separate meaning-ingestion proofs that parse real repo files into Form AST/container nodes, store those nodes inside the document meaning envelope, and query the stored ASTs at 1000 in both source and binary modes. document-bmf.fk adds a CFBF/OLE document-container decoder and document-meaning-ingestion-band.fk proves full-file DOC and PPT reads decode into queryable container facts such as sector shift, mini-sector shift, FAT sector count, and first directory sector at 5500 in source and binary modes. TypeScript kernel source attribution is now paired with Go/Rust through intern_node_at, node_source, and framebuffer-events so AST decoders can preserve provenance across all supported kernels. The remaining gap is explicit: the BMF byte cursor and first scanner-to-rule path exist and are paired across source and binary execution, but the active source compiler still has older string helper paths that need replacement by scanner-to-rule parsing before complete whole-repo structural query coverage is honest. context-lens-selection-band.fk still proves existing natural/image surface selection and roundtrip execution at 2490 in source and binary modes. Adjacent context-domain-media, natural bidirectional, and all-language bidirectional proofs remain paired at 1211, 7838, and 302 in source and binary modes.

phase_gate.reason: The phase gate stays false because CI and deploy validation are pending and the full .fk/.form migration is incomplete. The active direction is explicit and now underway: promote form.action from bootstrap text lowering to BMF-defined object rules, migrate Form source bodies into form.action/form.bml above the kernel, rebuild migrated files through BMF object rules, keep zero-arg functions supported in every sibling kernel, remove raw Lisp-style wrappers from active runtime tests, and maintain source plus binary parity proof for each migrated file.

proof_results: runtime-grammar-selector-registry-band.fk passes at 2100 in source and binary modes with runtime-grammar.fk loaded above engine.fk. The proof writes a config file mapping a file extension to a grammar .fk path, writes the grammar artifact separately, loads the grammar path through the config, registers the grammar plugin at runtime, parses a source file by filename extension, and verifies the parsed Form object. runtime-grammar.fk and the proof contain no Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust, image, audio, video, document, or natural-language selectors, and engine.fk contains no selector registry or filename discovery loader.

python-bmf-full-file-band.fk passes at 23100 in source and binary modes. It reads form-samples/test_module.py from disk, scans the full file into 46 Python BMF source objects with cursor spans, applies the Python sample-module BMF rule, verifies the rule consumes the complete stream with no rest, and checks the resulting module object for imports, from-import, constant assignment, function names, return binop, assignment call, and print call structure.

python-bmf-scanner-real-syntax-band.fk passes at 38500 in source and binary modes. python-bmf.fk now owns Python comments, single/double/triple-quoted strings, one- and two-letter string prefixes including fr, bytes, f-string and t-string source-object kinds, the broader Python 3.14 keyword/soft-keyword set, and a longest-op table covering decorators, @=, in-place ops, walrus, and ellipsis.

python-bmf-module-parse-band.fk passes at 38650 in source and binary modes. The Python parse entry now emits a generic py-module object, compact py-statement objects carrying kind/indent/CPython-rule name/token count, and an indentation-aware py-module-tree where form-samples/test_module.py has six top-level statements, def add owns one return child, and def main owns four indented children. Multiline parenthesized continuation remains one logical statement.

The Python file scanner path no longer reads the whole source file into a retained string. python-source-scan-file uses a file-backed cursor, fsc-source-cursor-char reads through a 4096-byte read_file_slice window, and source ranges store offset/length/start/end coordinates as lightweight Form lists instead of interning per-token text-line nodes.

Temporary real-repo proofs over Python files pass through python-parse-module-file after the file-backed scanner and collection changes: smallest 20 files at 20, files 21-50 at 30, and files 376-400 at 25. These are structural py-module parse proofs, not yet a claim of complete CPython grammar parity.

The sibling kernels now expose substrate mark/release as an optional scoped lifetime primitive and automatic mark-sweep substrate GC at execution boundaries. The heap/stack model is explicit: stack frames and closure environments are roots; the substrate heap contains interned recipes, string table entries, source attribution, and cache cells. substrate-gc-band.fk proves unreachable heap recipes are collected at 1000 in source and binary modes. substrate-cell-gc-band.fk proves live list cells keep referenced recipe nodes alive across GC at 5000 in source and binary modes. substrate-temp-arena-band.fk proves explicit scoped release returns temporary recipe/string counts to baseline at 15000 in source and binary modes.

The TypeScript kernel reader now decodes \r string escapes as carriage return, matching Go and Rust. The scanner parity probe proves fsc-space? treats a literal space as whitespace, literal r as non-whitespace, and r as a valid Python identifier-start character across all sibling kernels.
runtime-real-grammar-capsules-band.fk remains paired at 23100 in source and binary modes for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust, natural language, image, audio, video, and document grammar capsules.
